pr.title: WIP match ftNess_1, begin matching ftNess_2, complete ftNess_3
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: Large Ness fighter decomp PR that reorganized the old monolithic ftNess/ftness assembly and source into per-section files, matched or began matching ftNess_1 through ftNess_3 in C, and left later Ness code as split assembly stubs. The work added a new ftNess header with special attribute names, expanded Fighter/CollData/Hitbox/SmashAttr structure knowledge, and introduced a minimal item.h for yoyo item data needed by Ness's smash/yoyo logic.
what_changed: Deleted the old monolithic asm/melee/ft/chara/ftness.s and replaced it with split assembly files asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_2.s through ftNess_8.s.
Renamed src/melee/ft/chara/ftness.c to src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_1.c and updated its Ness special-attribute field access from a generic unk94 to x94_PSI_MAGNET_HEALING_MULTIPLIER.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_2.c with C for lbl_80114BF4 and func_80114C24, including the baseball bat item spawn path via func_802AD478, action state 0x155, x2248 storage, and damage/death callbacks.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_3.c, a large matched/decompiled block covering Ness forward smash baseball bat cleanup/reflect bubble callbacks and yoyo/up-smash/down-smash helper logic, with many decomp.me scratch links in comments.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess.h with ftNessAttributes laid out and named for PK Flash, PK Fire, PK Thunder, PSI Magnet, yoyo smash charge, and baseball bat reflection attributes.
Updated obj_files.mk to build ftNess_1.c, ftNess_2.c, ftNess_3.c, then the remaining ftNess_4.s through ftNess_8.s assembly slices instead of the old ftness.c plus ftness.s pair.

Expanded Fighter-related structs in src/melee/ft/fighter.h: Hitbox now exposes x4, x8, and xC damage fields; CollData gained Vec3 current/previous ECB vectors, flags, env flags, ground normal, wall vectors, and other offsets; SmashAttr was introduced at x2114; Ness special attributes now expose x222C, x2230 Vec3, x223C, and x2248 pointer-like fields.

Added a minimal src/melee/it/item.h defining ItemData, Item, and itYoyoAttributes so Ness yoyo code can access item extension attributes such as release velocity, gravity, pull strength, and string length.
Adjusted ftcommon.c and ftlib.c callers to use the new nested Fighter.x2114_SmashAttr fields instead of raw x2114/x2138-style fields.
smart_moves: Kept unmatched later Ness code as split assembly files while moving matched front portions into C, preserving build order and enabling incremental matching without blocking on the full character file.
Used obj_files.mk ordering to replace one monolithic object pair with a sequence of C and asm objects, a useful pattern for decompiling a long character file in chunks.

Named ftNessAttributes fields by gameplay behavior and offset, e.g. x94_PSI_MAGNET_HEALING_MULTIPLIER, xAC_SMASH_CHARGE_DURATION, xB0_SMASH_CHARGE_DAMAGE_MULTIPLIER, and xB8_REFLECTION_BUBBLE_BONE_ID, making later Ness special work more searchable.
Promoted raw Fighter offsets into reusable structures rather than scattering casts: SmashAttr at x2114, CollData fields used by yoyo collision, and Ness-specific sa.ness fields.
Introduced a small item.h only with the item/yoyo layout required by this work instead of trying to solve the entire item subsystem at once.
Used helper push_ecb to express repeated original-code behavior for CollData current/old ECB vector updates, documenting the suspected inlined pattern.
Applied #pragma dont_inline around functions where matching likely depended on call/inlining boundaries.
Preserved uncertainty in comments for unknown structs/fields rather than over-naming low-confidence data, for example the sp1C ECB array note and struct Unknown placeholder.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter files, splitting the asm remainder into numbered slices while matching early slices in C is a practical way to maintain linker/order correctness and reduce review scope.

When decompiling character-specific specials, useful struct progress often spans multiple systems: Fighter state vars, item extension attributes, collision data, hitboxes, and callbacks all needed new or refined headers here.
Field names that include both offset and behavior are valuable during early decomp because they preserve binary addressability while capturing gameplay meaning.

Ness yoyo and baseball bat logic uses many callback slots and state variables; mapping callback fields like x21E4_callback_OnDeath2, x21DC_callback_OnTakeDamage, x21C0, and x21BC is key to understanding object cleanup and hitbox updates.
Manual hitbox damage modification required exposing Hitbox.xC as f32 rather than leaving the whole hitbox as filler.

Collision helper decomp can be improved by recognizing repeated current-to-old vector update idioms and wrapping them in small helpers, but comments should note when the helper is an inferred/inlined pattern.
Smash charge state is better modeled as a nested SmashAttr struct at Fighter offset x2114, which also made unrelated ftcommon/ftlib callsites clearer.
Minimal subsystem headers can unblock matching: item.h exposed only ItemData, Item, and itYoyoAttributes needed by ftNess_3 rather than requiring complete item decomp.
naming_conventions: Character directory and files use camel-case ftNess/ftNess_N.c rather than the old lowercase ftness.c/ftness.s.

Ness special attributes use offset-prefixed descriptive names: x0_PKFLASH_TIMER1_MINCHARGEFRAMES, x20_AERIAL_LAUNCH_TRAJECTORY, x74_COOLDOWN_AFTER_RELEASE, xAC_SMASH_CHARGE_DURATION, xD8_REFLECTOR_BEHAVIOR.
Fighter nested fields keep offset prefixes plus semantic suffixes, e.g. x2114_SmashAttr.x2114_state and x2344_stateVar2_s32.
Unidentified functions and labels retain func_80xxxxxx/lbl_80xxxxxx names when no stronger symbol evidence is present.
Item yoyo attributes follow the same offset-plus-meaning pattern, e.g. x24_YOYO_RELEASE_VEL_X, x30_YOYO_RELEASE_VEL_Y, x34_GRAVITY.

Comments frequently label gameplay purpose next to still-unknown symbols, such as 'Remove Baseball Bat', 'Ness Yo-Yo Hitbox Position Update', and 'Apply modified damage to D-Smash Yo-Yo hitbox after charge'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted split-boundary markers in assembly such as 'SPLIT NO LATER THAN HERE' and 'SPLIT NO EARLIER THAN HERE' around difficult boundaries.
Preserved unmatched functions in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftNess/ftNess_4.s through ftNess_8.s while compiling ftNess_1.c through ftNess_3.c.
Used #pragma dont_inline on/off in ftNess_3.c around functions where inlining behavior affected matching.
Added local extern prototypes for not-yet-declared engine and item functions such as func_802AD478, func_802AD6B8, func_8007B23C, func_8004730C, and func_802BFE5C.

Used explicit magic constants where matching required exact floats or conversion behavior, including 0.0039059999398887157f, 0.5f, 1.5f, 60.0f, and magic conversion sdata2 constants in the asm remnants.
Modeled bitfield/flag manipulation through existing Fighter flag fields such as x2218_flag.bits.b0 and x2218_flag.bits.b3 when replacing raw rlwimi assembly patterns.
Kept decomp.me scratch links in comments for many functions, making matching provenance and future refinement easier to find.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_80114C24, func_80114F70, func_80114FF8, or other ftNess_3 symbols.
Check whether src/melee/it/item.h introduced here was later replaced or expanded by the main item subsystem headers.
Look for follow-up changes to CollData field names x4_vec, x1C_vec, x34_flags_1, x134_envFlags, and x154_groundNormal.
Search for later reviews or PRs refining ftNessAttributes names, especially yoyo and PSI Magnet unknown fields.
Identify the PR that matches ftNess_4 through ftNess_8 and compare whether the split boundaries from this PR were reused.
